# Issue 6 Feedback and Iteration Notes

## Ticket Context
- Ticket: 6
- Branch: feature/issue-6-floating-chat-widget
- Changed repository scope: InterviewCoach.WebUI and related tests/artifacts.

## Gaps Identified During Workflow
1. Manual interaction checks were initially vulnerable to false-positive interpretation when forced clicks were used.
2. Environment constraints (private NuGet feed 403) made fresh build validation unavailable at times, but no-build/manual runs could still appear partially successful.
3. Responsive verification in the browser harness can be inconclusive when viewport behavior is constrained.

## AI Artifact Updates Applied
1. Updated workflow prompt: [workflow/prompts/tidy-up.prompt.md](workflow/prompts/tidy-up.prompt.md)
- Added guardrail to prefer normal user-style UI interactions over forced clicks during manual verification.
- Added guardrail to treat stale/no-build verification as provisional only.

2. Updated workflow prompt: [workflow/prompts/implement-todo.prompt.md](workflow/prompts/implement-todo.prompt.md)
- Added UI verification guardrail against accepting force-only interaction success.
- Added instruction to keep todo checks open when fresh build/restore is blocked and only provisional verification exists.

## Why These Updates Matter
- Prevents masking real clickability/hit-target defects in future tickets.
- Improves trust in workflow sign-off by separating provisional evidence from production-ready evidence.
- Aligns implementation and tidy-up behavior with real-user interaction requirements.

## Remaining Risks for Issue 6
1. Minimize/close pointer interaction is still failing in live manual checks due interception behavior.
2. Full responsive viewport matrix remains blocked/inconclusive until fresh build + suitable harness validation is available.

## Recommendation for Next Iteration
1. Resolve mini-chat header click interception in code and re-run normal-pointer manual checks.
2. Re-run responsive matrix on a fresh build once NuGet/private-feed access is available.
3. Keep todo items for manual checks open until both are verified with non-provisional evidence.
